executive_summary.main_recommendation: Pain point first: monthly AI spend increased from 63679.11 USD in 2024-10 to 82808 USD in 2025-03, up 30% over the period, with increases in all 5 of 5 month-to-month periods and a peak in 2025-03 at 82808 USD. This means spend is rising steadily, so the next decision should separate controllable waste from good usage worth scaling. The clearest high-token, lower-delivery cost risks are Clara Chevalier, Adrien Ivanova, and Chloe Leroy: tokens per story point means how many tokens were spent to deliver one story point, and lower is better; Clara is at 38082437 versus the team median 17158730, Adrien at 30473430, and Chloe at 26678744, so cap usage, review prompt logs, reduce oversized context, and retrain them before approving more budget. The best patterns to replicate are Zoe Laurent and Remy Dupuis: story points per million tokens means how much delivery came from one million tokens, and higher is better; Zoe is at 0.14 versus the team median 0.06 and Remy is at 0.08, so recognize them and have them coach peers on prompt discipline, context selection, and task breakdown. The low-adoption, low-delivery group is Simon Martin, Quentin Morin, and Lucas Renard; Simon used 107222222 tokens for 14 story points, Quentin 172444444 for 19, and Lucas 550666666 for 28, so this is better handled with adoption coaching and workflow enablement than with budget cuts, then watch whether story points and tickets resolved improve next month. Overall, AI spend looks mixed rather than fully wasteful: there is clear waste to control in the overspender group, clear value to scale from the efficient users, and under-adoption in part of the team that may be limiting delivery gains.

employee_metrics.name: Zoe Laurent
employee_metrics.recommendation: Zoe Laurent is the strongest model to copy: 62 story points with 456666666 tokens, and 7365591 tokens per story point versus the team median 17158730. Story points per million tokens is 0.14 versus the team median 0.06, and she also resolved 9 tickets with a faster 1.56 days versus 2.56 days median. Praise this behavior and ask her to mentor peers on prompt discipline, smaller context, and how she turns AI use into finished work. Next month, watch whether tokens per story point stays below the team median while story points remain above 40.

employee_metrics.name: Remy Dupuis
employee_metrics.recommendation: Remy Dupuis shows a repeatable usage pattern: 45 story points with 564555555 tokens, or 12545679 tokens per story point versus the team median 17158730. He also delivered 0.08 story points per million tokens versus the team median 0.06, resolved 11 tickets versus 7 median, and finished work in 2 days versus 2.56 days median. Praise this behavior and have him coach others on prompt reuse, context selection, and task patterns that keep token use controlled. Next month, watch whether story points per million tokens stays above 0.06 while tickets resolved stay above 7.

employee_metrics.name: Alexandre Diallo
employee_metrics.recommendation: Alexandre Diallo is close to the team middle: 33 story points with 557555555 tokens, and 16895623 tokens per story point versus the team median 17158730. His tickets resolved match the team median at 7, but merge requests are 3 versus the team median 8, so there is room to tighten how work moves to completion. Monitor him and apply the efficient-user playbook if token use rises without a matching increase in story points or tickets resolved. Next month, watch whether story points move above 40 without tokens per story point rising above the team median.

employee_metrics.name: Alix Mbaye
employee_metrics.recommendation: Alix Mbaye is steady but not yet a model to copy: 42 story points with 720666666 tokens, exactly at the team median 17158730 tokens per story point. Delivery is solid with 10 tickets resolved versus 7 median and 2.3 days completion versus 2.56 days median. Monitor usage and retrain on the habits used by Zoe Laurent and Remy Dupuis so more delivery comes from the same token level. Next month, watch whether tokens per story point drops below the team median while story points stay at or above 42.

employee_metrics.name: Fatima Muller
employee_metrics.recommendation: Fatima Muller is a strong user worth recognizing: 52 story points with 688888888 tokens, or 13247863 tokens per story point versus the team median 17158730. She also resolved 10 tickets versus 7 median and completed work in 2.1 days versus 2.56 days median, showing a good balance of token use and delivery. Praise this pattern and ask her to mentor peers on how she structures work and prompts to keep token use efficient. Next month, watch whether story points stay above 40 while tokens per story point remains below the team median.

employee_metrics.name: Leila Park
employee_metrics.recommendation: Leila Park is slightly better than the team middle: 42 story points with 662777777 tokens, and 15780423 tokens per story point versus the team median 17158730. She matches the median on tickets resolved at 7 and finishes faster at 2 days versus 2.56 days median. Monitor her and replicate the efficient-user workflow if she can keep delivery rising without increasing tokens. Next month, watch whether story points stay above 40 while tokens used stays near or below 662777777.

employee_metrics.name: Nina Traore
employee_metrics.recommendation: Nina Traore is using more tokens than the team median but getting less delivery per token: 760000000 tokens for 34 story points, or 22352941 tokens per story point versus the team median 17158730. She resolved 9 tickets, but completion time is slower at 3 days versus 2.56 days median, so cost should be controlled until usage becomes more efficient. Retrain her on smaller context and tighter prompt scope, and monitor closely before allowing higher AI use. Next month, watch whether tokens per story point falls toward the team median while story points rise above 40.

employee_metrics.name: Victor Durand
employee_metrics.recommendation: Victor Durand is near the team median but slightly behind on efficiency: 40 story points with 702888888 tokens, or 17572222 tokens per story point versus the team median 17158730. He resolved 6 tickets versus 7 median and has only 3 merge requests versus 8 median, so the issue may be how work is being finished and shipped. Monitor him and retrain on efficient-user habits to improve delivery from similar token use. Next month, watch whether tickets resolved rise to at least 7 while tokens per story point moves below the team median.

employee_metrics.name: Lucas Renard
employee_metrics.recommendation: Lucas Renard looks under-adopted rather than simply wasteful: 28 story points with 550666666 tokens, below the team medians of 40 story points and 662777777 tokens. His tokens per story point is 19666667, above the team median 17158730, and completion time is 3.83 days versus 2.56 days median, so he needs help using AI more effectively in daily work. Run adoption coaching instead of cutting budget, because better usage could raise delivery more than a small cost reduction would. Next month, watch whether story points rise above 28 and tickets resolved rise above 6.

employee_metrics.name: Quentin Morin
employee_metrics.recommendation: Quentin Morin is a good case for enablement: he used only 172444444 tokens and delivered 19 story points, both well below the team medians of 662777777 tokens and 40 story points. His tokens per story point is 9076023 versus the team median 17158730, which means when he does use tokens, the conversion is good, but overall usage is too low to lift delivery enough. Run adoption coaching and give him examples from efficient users, because more effective use may create more value than budget cuts. Next month, watch whether story points rise above 19 while tokens used increases in a controlled way.

employee_metrics.name: Simon Martin
employee_metrics.recommendation: Simon Martin is also under-adopted: 107222222 tokens and 14 story points are both far below the team medians of 662777777 and 40. His tokens per story point is 7658730 versus the team median 17158730, so the issue is not waste per unit of delivery; it is that AI use is too limited to move enough work. Run adoption coaching rather than reducing access, and show him how efficient users apply AI to more tasks across the month. Next month, watch whether tickets resolved rise above 3 and story points rise above 14.

employee_metrics.name: Julien Bourgeois
employee_metrics.recommendation: Julien Bourgeois should not receive more AI budget yet: 597111111 tokens produced only 16 story points, or 37319444 tokens per story point versus the team median 17158730. He also resolved 3 tickets versus 7 median and took 3.33 days versus 2.56 days median, so current spend is not turning into enough delivered work. Retrain him on task breakdown, add review checkpoints, and monitor before allowing any increase in usage. Next month, watch whether tokens per story point drops materially while tickets resolved rises above 3.

employee_metrics.name: Adrien Ivanova
employee_metrics.recommendation: Adrien Ivanova is a clear cost-control case: he used 1401777777 tokens, the highest in the group, for 46 story points, which is 30473430 tokens per story point versus the team median 17158730. He does resolve many tickets at 12, but the token cost is too high, and completion time is slower than median at 2.92 days versus 2.56 days. Reduce or cap usage, review prompt logs, and retrain on smaller context so cost comes down before any budget increase. Next month, watch whether tokens per story point falls toward the team median while story points stay at or above 46.

employee_metrics.name: Chloe Leroy
employee_metrics.recommendation: Chloe Leroy is also a cost-reduction priority: 1227222222 tokens for 46 story points equals 26678744 tokens per story point versus the team median 17158730. Her delivery is decent, but the token cost is too high for the output, and 7 merge requests is below the team median 8. Cap usage, retrain on tighter prompts and smaller context, and monitor closely because cost should be reduced until delivery per token improves. Next month, watch whether tokens per story point drops below 26678744 while story points stay at or above 40.

employee_metrics.name: Clara Chevalier
employee_metrics.recommendation: Clara Chevalier is the strongest overspend signal: 1180555555 tokens produced 31 story points, or 38082437 tokens per story point versus the team median 17158730. She is using far more tokens than the median but delivering fewer story points than the median 40, so cost should be controlled immediately. Reduce or cap usage, review prompt logs, and retrain on smaller context and better prompt discipline before approving more spend. Next month, watch whether tokens per story point drops sharply while story points improve above 31.
